verses.1: When Israel went out of Egypt, the house of Jacob from a people of a foreign language;
verses.2: Judah was his sanctuary, and Israel his dominion.
verses.3: The sea saw it , and fled: Jordan was driven back.
verses.4: The mountains skipped like rams, and the little hills like lambs.
verses.5: What ailed thee, O thou sea, that thou fleddest? thou Jordan, that thou wast driven back?
verses.6: Ye mountains, that ye skipped like rams; and ye little hills, like lambs?
verses.7: Tremble, thou earth, at the presence of the LORD, at the presence of the God of Jacob;
verses.8: Who turned the rock into a standing water, the flint into a fountain of waters.

metadata.source: Webster's Bible (1833) — presented as-is with minor editorial corrections for the ABIDE Bible App.
